subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Feathered Friend
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Sven Olsen was a skilled construction worker in a space station. He was thin and lightweight, which made him suitable for working in space. Sven was known for carefully collecting floating girders and fixing them together in the weightless environment.
questions: Who was Sven Olsen?
Where did he work?
Why was Sven suitable for working in space?
What kind of worker was he?
What did he do with the floating girders?

passages.paragraph: Life inside the space station was very different from life on Earth. There was almost no gravity, so objects and people floated freely in the air. Workers had to move carefully and use special equipment to complete their tasks.
questions: Where did the workers live?
How was life in the space station different from Earth?
What happened to objects in zero gravity?
How did people move in the station?
What did workers need to complete their tasks?

passages.paragraph: One day the narrator heard a musical whistle near his ear. At first he thought the sound came from the station’s intercom. When he looked up, he saw a small yellow bird floating gracefully in the air.
questions: What sound did the narrator hear?
What did he first think the sound was?
What did he see when he looked up?
What color was the bird?
How was the bird moving?

passages.paragraph: The bird was a small canary named Claribel. Sven Olsen had secretly brought her to the space station. At first the crew was surprised, but soon they became fond of the cheerful little bird.
questions: What was the bird’s name?
What kind of bird was Claribel?
Who brought Claribel to the station?
How did the crew feel at first?
How did they feel later?

passages.paragraph: Claribel quickly adjusted to the weightless environment of the station. She floated smoothly through the air and even performed loops while flying. The crew enjoyed watching her graceful movements.
questions: How did Claribel adjust to the environment?
What kind of environment was the station?
How did Claribel move through the air?
What special movement did she perform?
How did the crew feel while watching her?

passages.paragraph: One morning the crew found Claribel lying motionless. They quickly gave her oxygen using a mask. After receiving oxygen, the bird showed signs of recovery, but the incident worried everyone on the station.
questions: What happened to Claribel one morning?
What did the crew do immediately?
What did they use to help the bird?
Did Claribel recover at first?
How did the crew feel after the incident?

passages.paragraph: The narrator suddenly remembered that canaries were once used in coal mines. Miners carried them underground because the birds were very sensitive to poisonous gases. If the bird became weak, it meant the air was dangerous.
questions: What did the narrator remember?
Where were canaries used earlier?
Why did miners carry canaries?
What happened if the bird became weak?
What did this indicate about the air?

passages.paragraph: After thinking carefully, the crew realized that the air purifier in the station had malfunctioned. The device that cleaned the air had stopped working properly. Because of this problem, the oxygen level in the station was becoming unsafe.
questions: What did the crew realize?
What device had malfunctioned?
What was the purpose of the device?
What problem did the malfunction cause?
What happened to the oxygen level?

passages.paragraph: The crew quickly checked the system and discovered that the alarm circuit had not yet been connected. Because of this, the warning system had failed to alert them about the dangerous air condition.
questions: What did the crew check?
What did they discover?
What system had not been connected?
What was the purpose of the alarm?
Why did the warning system fail?

passages.paragraph: Claribel’s sudden collapse helped the crew discover the dangerous problem in time. Without the little bird’s warning, the crew might not have noticed the danger. The small canary became a true 'feathered friend' to everyone on the station.
questions: What helped the crew discover the danger?
Who warned the crew?
What might have happened without the bird?
What did the crew call Claribel?
Why was Claribel important to the crew?
